content:
JavaScript is not fast because it does everything at once. It scales because it refuses to waste its main thread waiting for slow things.

12 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Most developers hear “single-threaded” and immediately imagine weakness.
One thread.
One call stack.
One thing at a time.
So how does the same runtime power APIs, real-time dashboards, chat apps, streaming systems, serverless functions, and backend services handling millions of requests?
The answer is not magic.
It is not because JavaScript secretly became multi-threaded.
It is because most backend work is not CPU work. It is waiting.
Waiting for databases. Waiting for Redis. Waiting for file systems. Waiting for third-party APIs. Waiting for network responses. Waiting for the cloud to answer.
And JavaScript, especially Node.js, is very good at not standing there like an intern staring at a loading spinner.
Single-Threaded Does Not Mean Weak
